title: Thorough Code Review
description: Review a diff for correctness, design, security, and maintainability.
content:
Review the following change:

```
{{diff}}
```

Go in this order, reporting only real issues:
1. Correctness — bugs, broken edge cases, wrong assumptions.
2. Security — injection, authz, secrets, unsafe input.
3. Design — coupling, naming, fit with existing patterns.
4. Maintainability — clarity, tests, docs.

For each finding give: severity, the specific location, why it matters, and a concrete fix. Lead with the most important. If it's solid, say so plainly rather than inventing nits.

variables.name: diff
variables.description: The diff or code to review

title: Security-Focused Review
description: Audit a change specifically for security vulnerabilities.
content:
Review this code for security issues only:

```
{{code}}
```

Check for: injection (SQL/command/template), broken authn/authz, secrets in code or logs, unsafe deserialization, SSRF, path traversal, missing input validation, and unsafe defaults. For each issue: the exploit scenario, severity, and the fix. If you find nothing exploitable, state what you verified rather than padding the list.

title: Review for Performance
description: Spot performance problems in a change before they ship.
content:
Review this code for performance problems:

```
{{code}}
```

Look for: N+1 queries, unnecessary allocations in hot paths, accidental O(n^2), blocking I/O on critical paths, missing pagination/limits, and repeated work that could be cached. For each, estimate the impact and whether it matters at this code's expected scale. Don't micro-optimize cold paths.

title: Review My Own PR
description: Self-review a change before requesting human review.
content:
Act as a demanding reviewer for my own change before I send it:

```
{{diff}}
```

Tell me what a sharp reviewer would push back on: unclear naming, missing tests, untested edge cases, scope creep, leftover debug code, or a simpler approach I missed. Be the critic I'd rather hear from now than in review. End with the single most important thing to fix first.

title: Explain Review Feedback
description: Understand and act on review comments you received.
content:
I got this review feedback and want to understand it fully:

{{feedback}}

On this code:

```
{{code}}
```

Explain what the reviewer is really concerned about, why it matters, and show the change that addresses it. If the feedback is debatable, give me the counter-argument too so I can respond thoughtfully.

title: Check Naming and API Design
description: Critique the names and public interface of a module.
content:
Critique the naming and public API of this code:

```
{{code}}
```

For names: are they accurate, consistent, and at the right level of abstraction? For the API: is it hard to misuse, minimal, and consistent with the rest of the codebase? Suggest better names and signatures where warranted, and explain the principle behind each suggestion. Flag any name that lies about what it does.

title: Review for Backward Compatibility
description: Check whether a change breaks existing callers or data.
content:
Assess whether this change is backward compatible:

```
{{diff}}
```

Identify any break to: public function signatures, serialized data formats, config keys, API responses, or behavior existing callers depend on. For each break, propose either a non-breaking alternative or a migration path (deprecation window, version gate, data migration). State clearly whether this can ship without a major version bump.

title: Concurrency Review
description: Review code for race conditions and concurrency bugs.
content:
Review this code for concurrency correctness:

```
{{code}}
```

Identify shared mutable state and the invariants on it. Look for: data races, check-then-act races, deadlock/lock-ordering issues, missing memory visibility, and unsafe lazy initialization. For each, describe the interleaving that triggers it and the fix. If it's a single-threaded context, confirm that and stop.

title: Review for Test Quality
description: Assess whether a change is adequately tested.

title: Review a Database Migration
description: Catch problems in a schema migration before it ships.

title: Review for Accessibility
description: Check a UI change for accessibility issues.

title: Assess Change Risk
description: Gauge how risky a change is and what to do about it.

title: Review Error Handling
description: Check that failures are handled, not just the happy path.

title: Review for Maintainability
description: Judge how this code will age, not just whether it works.

title: Review a Public API Change
description: Scrutinize a change to an interface others depend on.

title: Spot Hidden Coupling
description: Find the implicit dependencies a change introduces.
